cautions.text: Current transient dockage pricing should be confirmed directly.

standard.notes: Official transient page confirms fuel dock, pumpout, showers, laundry, internet access, and pool; current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

standard.rate_basis: transient dockage available; current rates direct
standard.notes: Waterway Guide lists 7.0 / 7.0 ft approach and dockside depth, 1.5 ft tide range, largest vessel 120 ft, and 20 transient slips. Dockage rates are not published; please contact the marina directly for pricing.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: Sail to the Sun Logbook: One of the bigger and more fully featured western-Bay marinas, with strong resort amenities, serious yard capability, and clear appeal for larger boats and longer stops. This one is genuinely big-boat friendly, with published docking for boats up to 120 feet.

local_notes.herrington_north_specials: Waterway Guide Specials: saltwater pool with kids pool, hot tub and pool bar, complimentary kayaks, paddleboards and bikes, indoor and outdoor fitness equipment, high-speed Wi-Fi, dog park, shaded community grill areas, spa-quality showers and restrooms, social events, playground, conference room, yard games, private security, West Marine, year-round haul and launch, multiple Travelifts, bottom paint services, dockside restaurant bar and grill, and an inn less than one mile away.

standard.rate_basis: in-season Sunday-Thursday per ft per night; Friday/Saturday/holidays $4.40/ft; off-season $2.50/ft
standard.notes: Waterway Guide lists 7.0 / 7.0 ft approach and dockside depth, 1.5 ft tide range, largest vessel 80 ft, and 30 transient slips. Official transient page lists in-season Sunday-Thursday at $3.50/ft, Friday/Saturday/holidays at $4.40/ft, off-season at $2.50/ft, with $8 for 30A and $16 for 50A electric per night.

cautions.text: Current transient dockage pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: Sail to the Sun Logbook: Herrington Harbour South is the more resort-style of the two Herrington marinas, with fuel, pump-out, beach, pool, deli, restaurant, and strong transient amenities. It is a very good weather-wait or comfort stop on the western Bay.

local_notes.herrington_south_specials: Waterway Guide / official transient amenities: private sandy beaches with kayaks and paddleboards, Olympic-size pool and kiddie pool, Ketch 22, Chesapeake Market and Deli, complimentary Wi-Fi, full-service fuel dock with pump-out stations, fitness center, lighted tennis court, basketball, pickleball, playground, picnic areas with grills, and restrooms, showers, and laundry.

standard.rate_basis: 31 ft and over daily transient rate; 30 ft or less is $3.00/ft daily
standard.electric_rate_basis: electric included in seasonal transient rates per official page
standard.water_rate_basis: water included in seasonal transient rates per official page
standard.notes: Official transient page lists seasonal transient daily rates of $3.00/ft for boats 30 ft or less and $3.25/ft for 31 ft and over; weekly and monthly rates also published.

cautions.text: This is the downtown Annapolis convenience play, so expect more harbor traffic and activity than at quieter outlying marinas.

expert_notes.plain: Sail to the Sun Logbook: Your cruising editor once lived aboard here while working for Waterway Guide when they were based out of Annapolis, and loved it. It is convenient to some of Annapolis’s best pubs and restaurants, with easy access to and from the Bay, and a short walk to downtown — and an easy stagger back. Transient dockage is available, but you will need to book ahead, especially during the fall Sailboat Show.

local_notes.annapolis_context: Sail to the Sun Logbook: Annapolis is America’s sailing capital, and you will know it the moment you cruise into town. If there is something you need, you will find it here, whether it is from Bacon’s, https://baconsails.com/, the quintessential consignment shop, one of the boutique marine stores, or the many competent yachtyards. If you are looking for crabcakes, Davis’ Pub is your best stop. Good burgers too. During the show, do not be surprised to see high-profile sailors around town. It is Annapolis. If you as a sailor do not feel right at home in Annapolis, you will never feel at home anywhere.

standard.rate_basis: nightly and monthly transient slips available; current rates direct
standard.notes: Official site confirms transient dockage, only fuel in Back Creek, water taxi to downtown, showers/laundry, and high-speed diesel pump; current rates should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: Current transient dockage pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A very strong Annapolis transient answer, especially for cruisers who want fuel, Back Creek protection, and easy water-taxi access to downtown.

source.name: North East River Marina official site

standard.rate_basis: transient slips often available by reservation
standard.notes: Official site confirms transient slips often available by reservation; current rates and utilities should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: Transient services, pricing, and utility details should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A useful upper-Bay transient-by-reservation option near the C&D approach, especially if you value protection over broad amenities.

standard.rate_basis: dock up to 45 ft while dining or visiting; current overnight/transient details direct
standard.notes: Official page emphasizes over 50 slips and the restaurant destination role; current transient and overnight details should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: Current transient/overnight terms should be confirmed directly because the official page leans heavily toward restaurant and general marina use.

expert_notes.plain: A useful upper-Bay stop that is as much a dining destination as a marina. Better thought of as a nice stopover option than a full-service cruiser base.

standard.rate_basis: per ft weekdays (Sunday-Thursday) overnight dockage; weekends and holidays $4.00/ft
standard.notes: Hourly first-come transient dockage before 5 p.m. is $10/hr up to 50 ft and $15/hr over 50 ft. Overnight minimum is $40. Call 410-263-7973 or hail VHF 17 for space assignment. Construction at City Dock means many slips are closed and some water/power hookups may be suspended.

cautions.text: City Dock resiliency construction has closed many slips, so space is constrained and some water/power hookups may be suspended.
This is the middle of the Annapolis circus. Expect traffic, activity, and attention.

expert_notes.plain: Sail to the Sun Logbook: If you want to be right in the middle of Annapolis, this is it. Great for instant access to town, but it is not the quiet choice.

geo.segment: Annapolis / Spa Creek and Back Creek

standard.rate_basis: transient moorings first-come, first-served
standard.notes: Transient moorings cannot be reserved. Front Forty (#1-40) is $35/night for vessels up to 55 ft. Saint Mary's Cove (#41-60) is $25/night for vessels up to 35 ft. Well's Cove (#61-66), Truxtun Park (#67-71), and Back Creek (#72-76) are $35/night for vessels up to 55 ft. The seventh night is free if paid in advance for a week.

cautions.text: Transient city moorings are first-come, first-served and cannot be reserved.

expert_notes.plain: Sail to the Sun Logbook: If you want Annapolis without paying full dockage, the city moorings are a very real answer — especially if you are comfortable using the water taxi or your dinghy.

standard.rate_basis: transient dockage confirm direct
standard.notes: Official site emphasizes 250 deep-water slips, pool, and haul-out facilities; current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A protected Deale-area marina with strong yard capability and a good weather-wait profile on the western shore.

standard.rate_basis: daily, weekly, monthly transient slips and moorings
standard.notes: Official site says slips have drafts up to 7 ft and transient slips accommodate boats up to 85 ft; current rates should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A classic Galesville / West River protected stop with both slips and moorings, especially appealing if you value shelter more than instant fuel access.

source.name: Marina at Nautilus Point official site / Waterway Guide

standard.rate_basis: in-season nightly per ft; boat shows and holidays $4.00/ft; off-season $2.00/ft
standard.notes: Official slip page lists nightly dockage at $3.00/ft in season, $4.00/ft during boat shows and holidays, and $2.00/ft off-season. Waterway Guide lists 153 total slips, 8 transient slips, and a largest vessel of 60 ft.

cautions.text: Boat show and holiday rates run higher and may carry minimum-stay requirements.

expert_notes.plain: A strong Eastport / Back Creek transient option with better pricing transparency than many Annapolis marinas and a good balance between protection and town access.

source.name: Bert Jabin Yacht Yard official site / Waterway Guide

standard.notes: Waterway Guide lists 9.5 / 9.0 ft approach and dockside depth, largest vessel 75 ft, and 25 transient slips. Official site and marina listings confirm transient dockage/day-trip options. Dockage rates are not published; please contact the marina directly for pricing.

cautions.text: This is a serious yacht yard as much as a transient marina, which is an advantage if you need service but less of a resort stop.

expert_notes.plain: One of the stronger Back Creek working-marina answers if you want depth, protection, and real yard capability rather than a polished resort feel.
